title: The Power of Small Habits
intro_zh: 本文约1000词，探讨如何通过培养小习惯实现个人成长。适合四级水平阅读，包含15个核心词汇。

paragraphs.en: Have you ever wondered why some people seem to achieve so much while others struggle to make progress? The secret may lie not in grand goals, but in the small habits we practice every day. A single small action might not seem important, but over time it can lead to remarkable results.
paragraphs.zh: 你是否曾好奇，为什么有些人似乎成就斐然，而另一些人却难以取得进步？秘密可能不在于宏大的目标，而在于我们每天践行的小习惯。单个小行动可能看起来微不足道，但随着时间的推移，它能带来惊人的结果。

paragraphs.en: Our brains love patterns. When we repeat an action enough times, it becomes automatic. Think about brushing your teeth or riding a bike. You don't have to think hard about each step; your brain just does it. This is how habits work: they save mental energy and make good behaviors easier over time.
paragraphs.zh: 我们的大脑喜欢模式。当我们重复一个动作足够多次，它就会变成自动的。想想刷牙或骑自行车。你不必费力思考每一步；大脑自动完成。这就是习惯的运作方式：它们节省脑力，让好的行为随时间推移变得更轻松。

paragraphs.en: However, forming a new habit can be challenging. Many people try to make huge changes all at once and quickly become discouraged. The key is to start small. For example, if you want to read more, begin with just one page a day. This tiny step is so easy that you won't make excuses.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，养成新习惯可能具有挑战性。许多人试图一次性做出巨大改变，很快就变得气馁。关键在于从小处着手。例如，如果你想多读书，从每天只读一页开始。这个小步骤太容易了，以至于你不会找借口。

paragraphs.en: One effective strategy is to connect a new habit with an existing one. This is called "habit stacking." For instance, after you pour your morning coffee, you might do two minutes of stretching. The old habit reminds you to do the new one, making it easier to stick to your plan.
paragraphs.zh: 一个有效的策略是将新习惯与现有习惯联系起来。这被称为“习惯叠加”。例如，早上倒完咖啡后，你可以做两分钟的伸展运动。旧习惯提醒你做新习惯，让你更容易坚持计划。

paragraphs.en: Another principle is to make the habit attractive. People are more likely to do something they enjoy. If you dislike running, try listening to your favorite music while jogging. Or pair the habit with a treat, like watching an episode of a show only while on the treadmill. This creates a positive feeling that reinforces the behavior.
paragraphs.zh: 另一个原则是让习惯有吸引力。人们更可能做自己喜欢的事情。如果你不喜欢跑步，试着在慢跑时听最喜欢的音乐。或者将习惯与奖励配对，比如只在跑步机上时看一集节目。这会创造一种积极的感觉，强化该行为。

paragraphs.en: Environment also plays a big role. If you want to eat healthier, keep fruits and vegetables in plain sight and hide junk food. By designing your surroundings to make good choices easy and bad ones hard, you reduce the need for willpower.
paragraphs.zh: 环境也起着重要作用。如果你想吃得更健康，就把水果和蔬菜放在显眼的地方，把垃圾食品藏起来。通过设计环境，让好的选择变得容易、坏的选择变得困难，你就能减少对意志力的需求。

paragraphs.en: It's also important to track your progress. Use a calendar and mark each day you complete your habit. Seeing a chain of X's can be highly motivating. Even if you miss a day, don't break the chain for two days in a row. The goal is to be consistent, not perfect.
paragraphs.zh: 跟踪自己的进步也很重要。使用日历，每天完成习惯后做一个标记。看到一连串的叉号会非常有动力。即使错过一天，也不要连续两天中断。目标是坚持，而非完美。

paragraphs.en: Finally, remember that habits are like compounds of interest. The benefits accumulate over time. A small improvement of just 1% each day leads to being 37 times better after a year. So start today, be patient, and let your small habits build a better future.
paragraphs.zh: 最后，记住习惯就像复利。益处随时间积累。每天仅仅1%的小改进，一年后就会带来37倍的提升。所以今天就开始，保持耐心，让小习惯为你构建更美好的未来。

paragraphs.en: In short, don't underestimate the power of small habits. They may seem minor, but their long-term effect is enormous. Whether you want to improve your health, learn a new skill, or become more productive, start with one tiny habit today. Your future self will thank you.
paragraphs.zh: 简而言之，不要低估小习惯的力量。它们看起来或许微不足道，但长期影响是巨大的。无论你想改善健康、学习新技能还是提高效率，从今天的一个微小习惯开始。未来的你将会感谢自己。
